findings: Strong spiral structure: M01 PRAO -> M03 Trace Reading -> M04 TCEF -> M07 Skills -> M12 Capstone forms a coherent learning arc
Modules 04-09 show a content depth cliff (6144-5757 words vs 8202-10412 for M01-M03), suggesting incomplete build-out
Module 12 capstone effectively synthesizes all prior modules with explicit back-references (45 cross-refs)
M09 now introduces runtime surfaces that connect Claude Code orchestration to SDK/API deployment paths, reducing the CLI-only framing gap
The PRAO framework provides excellent structural backbone across all modules

top_issues.description: Modules 04-09 average 5,281 words vs 9,657 for M01-M03 — content depth inconsistency suggests incomplete development
top_issues.recommendation: Expand M04-M09 to match the depth and interactive richness of M01-M03

top_issues.description: No explicit module on Claude SDK programmatic API (claude.ai SDK, agent builder patterns)
top_issues.recommendation: Consider adding SDK coverage to M09 or M12, or a dedicated module

top_issues.description: Module 08 (Meta-Prompting) at 4,267 words is the thinnest module, yet covers a complex topic
top_issues.recommendation: Expand with more concrete meta-prompting examples and evaluation exercises

findings: Predict-before-explain pattern used consistently across all 12 modules — excellent pedagogical practice
Every module opens with clear learning objectives via ix-diagram objective components
Deep Dive collapsibles provide optional depth without cognitive overload — well-designed progressive disclosure
Quiz questions align with learning objectives and use Bloom's taxonomy (apply/analyze level, not just recall)
The 'Senior Engineer on Day One' test in M07 is an exemplary skill assessment heuristic

top_issues.description: Interactive element density drops significantly in M04-M09 (avg 11.5 elements vs 22.3 for M01-M03). Students may disengage.
top_issues.recommendation: Add more agent-trace, terminal-sim, and intervention components to maintain engagement

top_issues.description: Module 08 now has stronger quiz coverage, but practice remains light without a terminal-sim or guided builder exercise
top_issues.recommendation: Add a hands-on meta-prompt builder or terminal-sim to deepen practice

top_issues.description: Capstone module is content-heavy (11,138 words) but may overwhelm on Day 3 of intensive. No pacing guidance.
top_issues.recommendation: Add explicit time estimates per section and consider splitting into design + execution phases

findings: Zero violations of the 8 prohibited hallucinations (--thinking, --context, /memory, SSE-as-current, wrong primitive count, triggers activation)
SSE correctly identified as deprecated in M02, M05, M11 with proper Streamable HTTP guidance
Three MCP primitives (Tools, Resources, Prompts) consistently and correctly stated
triggers field in skill YAML correctly identified as LUXOR convention, not native Claude Code feature (M07, M11)
CLAUDE.md correctly described as read-at-session-start, not a command (M02, M07, M12)

top_issues.description: Module 12 back-reference precision was corrected in the implementation refresh; the remaining risk is future drift if module summaries change without an audit pass.
top_issues.recommendation: Keep the M12 cross-reference audit in the review checklist for future curriculum edits

top_issues.description: M02 references four tool categories (Files, Bash, Web, MCP) — Web tools (WebSearch, WebFetch) may not be available in all Claude Code configurations
top_issues.recommendation: Add a note that Web tools availability depends on the Claude Code plan/configuration

top_issues.description: MCP TypeScript SDK import paths use .js extensions (e.g., server/mcp.js) which is correct for ESM but may confuse TypeScript newcomers
top_issues.recommendation: Add a brief note explaining ESM .js extension convention in TypeScript

findings: Module 10 security coverage is strong: scope creep, secret exposure, prompt injection all addressed with concrete settings.json examples
Permission configurations for three deployment contexts (dev, read-only, CI) are production-realistic
Module 06 error handling covers isError flag pattern and timeout strategies
Module 12 pipeline archetypes (simple chain, fan-out, conditional router) map to real production patterns
M12 now covers token budgeting, concurrency caps, rate limiting, and circuit-breaker style cost control

top_issues.description: Cost controls now exist in M12, but the course still lacks a worked budget dashboard example with concrete telemetry screenshots or sample metrics
top_issues.recommendation: Add one end-to-end production cost and observability example showing budgets, burn rate, and alert thresholds

top_issues.description: Observability section mentions structured logging but does not cover OpenTelemetry integration, tracing correlation IDs, or cost dashboards
top_issues.recommendation: Expand observability guidance with concrete tool recommendations

top_issues.description: M10 now includes code-level mitigation patterns, but additional scenario-driven exercises would make the defenses easier to operationalize under pressure
top_issues.recommendation: Add one or two interactive injection-defense scenarios to the security lab or module exercises

findings: Consistent voice across all 12 modules: professional, direct, second-person address
Terminology is consistent: PRAO, TCEF, Agentic Loop used uniformly with the same definitions
Module transitions are smooth — each module opens by connecting to prior concepts
Double-dash (--) used consistently instead of em-dash across all modules
Jargon is introduced before use in every case observed

top_issues.description: Module 04 title says 'Prompt Engineering Depth' but the module refers to both GCCF and TCEF frameworks — the transition from GCCF to TCEF could be clearer
top_issues.recommendation: Add explicit framing: 'GCCF is the baseline you already know; TCEF is the upgrade this module teaches'

top_issues.description: Module 12 overview mapping is now corrected; preserve that wording if the overview is revised again so the editor pass does not reintroduce the old mismatch
top_issues.recommendation: Keep the corrected M12 overview wording aligned with the module focus map during future edits

top_issues.description: Some modules use 'Agentic AI Engineering — 3-Day Intensive Course' and others use 'Agentic AI Engineering -- 3-Day Intensive Course' (em-dash vs double-dash inconsistency in subtitle)
top_issues.recommendation: Standardize to one dash convention throughout

findings: Component variety is excellent: 12+ distinct interactive component types used across the course
Hero sections with chip tags provide immediate module topic scannability
Click-cards and tabbed-panels enable self-paced exploration without forced linear reading
Agent-trace components with annotations are uniquely effective for teaching trace reading (M03)
Standalone HTML diagrams now cover all 12 modules, restoring visual support in the back half of the course

top_issues.description: Baseline diagram coverage is now restored for Modules 06-12, but each later module currently has only one standalone visual companion
top_issues.recommendation: Add second-wave visuals only where a module still has a dense concept cluster that would benefit from another interactive diagram

top_issues.description: Module 04 has the fewest interactive elements per word (11 elements / 6144 words = 1.8/1000w) vs M02 (24/10356 = 2.3/1000w)
top_issues.recommendation: Add more interactive components, especially agent-trace examples showing TCEF in action

top_issues.description: Module 08 (Meta-Prompting) lacks a terminal-sim or hands-on code block exercise — students have no chance to try meta-prompting
top_issues.recommendation: Add an interactive meta-prompt builder exercise

findings: The PRAO loop acts as a natural endofunctor: P->R->A->O->P, providing compositional structure for every module
Modules compose sequentially: M_n outputs (concepts, skills) become M_{n+1} inputs, forming a chain category
The TCEF framework is a clear functor from the category of tasks to the category of prompts
Natural transformation from theory to practice is visible: each concept has a corresponding interactive exercise
The M08->M09 bridge is now explicit: evaluated specialist artifacts become orchestrator inputs rather than isolated prompt outputs

top_issues.description: The bridge between M08 and M09 is now explicit, but it would be stronger with one full end-to-end lab that takes a generated artifact into orchestration
top_issues.recommendation: Add one worked example or lab exercise that carries a specialist artifact from meta-prompting into a multi-agent run

top_issues.description: No explicit monoidal structure for combining skills. Skills are introduced (M07) but skill composition/combination operators are not formalized.
top_issues.recommendation: Add skill composition patterns (sequential, parallel, conditional) to M07 or M09

top_issues.description: The concept prerequisite graph has a single orphaned concept: 'Context7' introduced in M11 with no prior mention in M02 or M05 where MCP servers are covered
top_issues.recommendation: Add a brief Context7 mention in M05 when discussing MCP server examples

orphaned: Approval gates (introduced M10, not reinforced in M12 capstone exercises)
assumed: JSON Schema (used in M05-M06 without formal introduction)
Zod (used in M06 SDK examples without introduction)
CI/CD pipelines (referenced in M02, M10, M12 but never explained)

recommendations.description: Expand Modules 04-09 to match M01-M03 depth and interactive density
recommendations.impact: Eliminates the content cliff in the middle of the course

recommendations.description: Add token economics coverage (cost management, context budgeting)
recommendations.impact: Addresses the #1 question senior engineers will ask
Maintains visual learning support consistency across the entire course
Improves learning flow through the most disconnected transition

recommendations.description: Add concrete prompt injection defense code examples to M10
Mention Context7 earlier in M05 MCP examples
recommendations.impact: Removes orphaned concept, creates smoother M05->M11 connection

recommendations.description: Add Claude SDK/API programmatic integration coverage
recommendations.impact: Closes the gap between CLI usage and production deployment

recommendations.description: Standardize dash convention (-- vs em-dash) in module subtitles
